rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Reinstating Order Approving Auction Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 16, PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Reinstating Order Approving Auction Notice. Hearing required. The court re-issues the tentative ruling that it issued prior to the April 12, 2018 hearing. Plaintiff Legal Recovery, LLC's motion for an order reinstating the January 16, 2018 order approving auction notice is treated as a motion to enter an order approving an auction notice with the same language as contained in the January 16 order and, so treated, is granted. Legal Recovery is entitled to an order approving an auction notice with the same language as contained in the January 16 order. Nor has defendant Martin Eng shown any reason why such an order should not be issued. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Legal Recovery is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint Pursuant To Cal. Code Of Civ. Proc. 426.50.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT KEARNY WASHINGTON'S LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint Pursuant To Cal. Code Of Civ. Proc. 426.50. Defendant Kearny Washington, LLC's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is denied. Kearny Washington lacks standing to vacate the judgment in the related case and, in all events, even if Kearny Washington had standing, the proposed cross-complaint is an impermissible collateral attack on the judgment. rulings.Case Title: JULIAN WYLER ET AL VS. PJ CON, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTs JOHN NERAREZ and PJ CON, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Defendants PJ Con, Inc. and John Nevarez's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Mike and Marija Jozinovic is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to all three causes of action. As to the breach of contract and common counts causes of action, the cross-complaint does not adequately allege the nature of the "services" or the "substance" of the contract's terms. In addition, there is insufficient basis alleged for Mr. Jozinovic basis for asserting a contract or common counts claim. At a minimum, Mr. Jazinovic needs to allege how he individually and the sole proprietorship entered into an agreement with PJ Con. The fraud cause of action should be alleged with more particularity. The Jozinovics should clarify that the representation was oral and what other projects they continued to work on because of the alleged falsehood. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS SONG VS. MICHAEL RABBITT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS MICHAEL RABBITT, MARGARET MULLIGAN, H.J. RABBITT & SONS INC. AKA HENRY J. RABBITT & SONS INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint. The Rabbit Defendants' motion for leave to file a first amended cross-complaint is granted and all litigation as to the new claims are stayed pending the conclusion of the trial on the current claims. While California's liberal policy allowing amendments to pleadings favors permitting the Rabbit Defendants to file their proposed first amended cross-complaint, including those claims in a trial that is scheduled to start in a month and a half is prejudicial to the parties against whom the new claims are alleged unless the trial is continued and continuing the trial is prejudicial to the plaintiffs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: KELLY KIMBALL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Xelan Prop 1, Llc As A Named Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF KELLY KIMBALL Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Xelan Prop 1, Llc As A Named Defendant. Plaintiff Kelly Kimball's motion for leave to amend complaint to add Xelan Prop 1, LLC as a named defendant is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy allowing amendments to pleadings and defendants have not identified any prejudice to them if the motion is granted. The argument that Xelan cannot be liable for any of the claims alleged by Mr. Kimball is best addressed on a demurrer or other pleading motion, not a motion to amend, although it appears that there is no legal impediment to imposing liability on Xelan merely because it is an entity rather than an individual. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Kimball is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON SALDAVIA VS. MACY'S CORPORATE SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses From Macys West Stores, Inc. Re Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 1, Plaintiff Sharon Saldavia's Motion To Compel Further Responses From Macys West Stores, Inc. Re Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to April 25, 2018. The Judge Pro Tem recuses herself. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY P. TORLAI JR. VS. PAMELA RAMIREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 17, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM ROBINSON, WILLIAM G ROBINSON, ACCOUNTANCY CORPORATION, PAMELA RAMIREZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Pamela Ramirez, William G. Robinson, and William G. Robinson Accountancy Corporation's demurrer to all causes of actions in the first amended complaint filed is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all causes of action. As to the claims against Ms. Ramirez, plaintiffs are given leave to amend to allege facts showing that the one-year statute of limitations per CCP 366.2(a) was tolled because they timely filed a creditor's claims and the executor failed to allow, approve, or reject the claims. When a cause of action appears to be barred by an applicable statute of limitations, plaintiffs must plead facts which show tolling to avoid the statutory bar. (Ponderosa Homes, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 1761, 1768.) The portions of the claims seeking damages based on Laura and Greg Torlai's tax years 2005, 2006, and 2007 and Stony Creek Land & Cattle Company, Inc.'s tax years ending March 2006 and March 2007 are time-barred by the two-year statute of limitations in CCP 339(1). Receipt of notices of deficiency more than two years prior to the filing of the complaint bars allegations of accounting malpractice, however labeled, for the years for which the notices were received. (Curtis v. Kellogg & Andelson (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 492, 500-501.) As to all other tax years for which plaintiffs seek damages, plaintiffs are given leave to allege precisely which tax years they are seeking damages and to allege that they did not receive a notice of deficiency for those tax years more than two years before this case was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and also complies with CCP 437c(g) and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SANDFORD TAYLOR VS. GLOBAL EXPERIENCE SPECIALISTS, INC. A NEVADA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 2, Defendant Global Experience Specialists, Inc.'s Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Discovery Responses. Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are ordered to appear. Defendant's Motion for Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Plaintiff's Deposition And Discovery Responses And Sanction Plaintiff In The Amount of $5,979.60 is unopposed and is granted in part. Plaintiff Sandford Taylor shall serve code-compliant verified responses to Request for Production of Documents (Set One) and Form Interrogatories (Set One) and appear for his deposition within 5 days. The Court notes the approaching trial date and defendant's pending motion to continue the trial date. However, defendant's argument that plaintiff, who is pro per, is acting willfully and with "utter disregard for this litigation and the rules of discovery" is not supported by evidence plaintiff has actually received the deposition notice, written discovery, or defendant's motion. Therefore the Court declines to award terminating sanctions at this time. The issue of monetary sanctions will be addressed at the hearing. Counsel for defendant Global Experience Specialists, Inc. shall notify plaintiff of this tentative ruling and the date, time and location of the hearing. Defendant should come prepared to discuss their last contact with plaintiff, efforts to contact plaintiff by telephone, and any effort to personally serve plaintiff. Plaintiff should come prepared to discuss if there have been changes in his street address or telephone number since he filed his Complaint and if so, any effort he made to notify defendant. Plaintiff shall also come prepared to provide dates certain for scheduling his deposition. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and bring the proposed order to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BARLOW VS. MINKANG ZHAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 19, 2018, Line 15, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER BARLOW'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Hearing required. =(302/HEK)
